Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Valinhall]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== House of Blades ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 6''' — Valinhall is a house that is also a Territory containing an entire world within its walls.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 6''' — Valinhall is a house that tests and attacks its inhabitants to teach them.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 6''' — Time flows slower in Valinhall than in the outside world.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 7''' — Simon settled into life in Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 8''' — Kai threatens to remove Simon from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 8''' — Simon feared for his life in the Valinhall House.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 8''' — Everything in Valinhall is hostile.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 9''' — Valinhall was once a world with cities, trees, and a sun before it was broken into a shard.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 9''' — Valinhall is a House that drifted into the nothing beyond all worlds.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 9''' — Valinhall is a Territory that differs from others in how Travelers use power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 10''' — Valinhall contains a long blue-lit basement.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 10''' — Valinhall's layout includes a garden of sky and open plains, a library, and a forge.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 11''' — Valinhall has a garden.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 11''' — Valinhall is an organization or house Kai intends to give himself to.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 11''' — Valinhall is a source of essence for Simon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 12''' — Simon has instincts born in Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 12''' — Simon possesses Traveler's gifts from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 13''' — Valinhall is located behind the entry hall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 13''' — Simon draws power from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 14''' — Simon cannot escape by Traveling through Valinhall because the Gate opens wherever you had last made it.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 15''' — Valinhall is a place Simon can reach through his sword.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 15''' — Caius was shoved into Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 18''' — Simon reaches out to Valinhall to call upon steel strength and Nye speed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 19''' — Simon possesses the strength and speed of Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 21''' — Traveling through Valinhall returns you to where you started.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Valinhall is a source of power that Indirial can call upon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 2''' — Valinhall is a location where weapons are stored and repaired by the Nye.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 2''' — Valinhall houses two women.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 3''' — Valinhall Travelers excel in open combat but lack versatility.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Valinhall provides power that allows certain individuals to wield Mithra.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Valinhall is a Territory that has a reputation for being useless for traveling quickly.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — A Gate opened from Valinhall will return to the same spot in the real world where it was opened.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — The stone temple at the center of the Badari Desert is a location one can return to from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Valinhall is a place the Wanderer would never choose to enter.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 5''' — Valinhall is a Territory that has existed for sixty years and was created as an experiment by Damasca.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 6''' — Simon traveled to Valinhall for healing.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 6''' — Valinhall was not equipped to support bears.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 6''' — Simon is a Traveler of Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 6''' — Simon has conquered Valinhall to a point.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 6''' — Simon thought he heard dolls laughing from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 7''' — Valinhall is a House.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 7''' — Valinhall contains a healing pool.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Angeline is part of Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Valinhall's powers only last for a certain amount of time.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 10''' — Valinhall is a location associated with certain Travelers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 12''' — The world is shaping itself around the Wanderer to become more like Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 12''' — The Incarnation seeks to make the world like Valinhall: harsh and unforgiving, full of combat.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 12''' — Valinhall is effective at destroying foreign Travelers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 14''' — The frozen horn was attuned to Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 14''' — Valinhall is a name mentioned by everyone.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 20''' — There is a time difference in Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 20''' — Valinhall is a location.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 20''' — Valinhall is associated with Incarnations and Travelers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 21''' — Valinhall is a location with a Gate.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 21''' — Valinhall is a place Simon might need to return to.}}

== City of Light ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 1''' — Valinhall contains a room meant for honoring the memory of those who died.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 2''' — Valinhall is a new Territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 3''' — Valinhall is a Territory that exists to kill other Travelers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 4''' — Valinhall contains a courtyard with gray stone columns and tiles.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 6''' — Time passes in Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 6''' — Kai is unable to leave Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 6''' — Valinhall is a place where constant vigilance is the highest virtue.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 8''' — Valinhall is a place one can reach out to for power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 8''' — Olissa suspects Simon's mask can draw more power from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 9''' — Valinhall contains an entry room with a wooden rack.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 10''' — Simon has become more used to lethal threats since moving to Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 10''' — Valinhall is a location from which Simon could have summoned items.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 11''' — Valinhall is associated with powers that Simon doubts can deal with levitation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 12''' — Valinhall is associated with new weapons.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 13''' — Lycus and Andra have lived in Valinhall for several months.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — Kai lives in Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — Valinhall is a location where an invasion occurred.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial walks into Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Valinhall is falling apart.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — Valinhall is a Territory that is good for fighting Travelers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — Valinhall can strengthen the body and fortify against pain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 19''' — Valinhall's powers are internal.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Valinhall is meant to challenge those with even more power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Valinhall is a way of reckoning time.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — King Zakareth believed the only colors of Valinhall were black and silver.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Valinhall is described as magnificent.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — The furniture in Valinhall can rise to defend it from outsiders.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Valinhall is a Territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 24''' — Queen Cynara recommends Valinhall to Leah.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 26''' — The King tried to destroy Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 26''' — Valinhall is a House that the King of Ragnarus has attacked.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 27''' — Rhalia is not familiar with Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 27''' — Simon does not have the chains of Valinhall on him.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 27''' — Simon can summon a blade from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 28''' — Simon is the leader of Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 28''' — A bond to the Valinhall Territory works both ways.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 28''' — To enjoy Valinhall's powers, one must pay their debts.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
